evals.prompt: Here is my PyTorch Lightning training code. Convert it to a FLARE federated job, run it with 3 simulated sites using FedAvg for 3 rounds, and show me validation results.
evals.expected_output: A FLARE Lightning Client API conversion that patches the trainer with flare.patch, uses standard client.py/job.py/model.py layout, a runnable job.py using the PyTorch FedAvg recipe with 3 simulated sites and 3 rounds, local validation evidence, metric evidence, and an export attempt under /tmp/nvflare/job_config when supported.
assertions: The agent runs nvflare agent inspect before editing.
The agent edits only training, model, job, or small config files.
The agent integrates through flare.patch(trainer) and lets the patched trainer own model exchange.
The agent does not generate a manual FLModel send/receive path as the default Lightning exchange.
The agent does not pass the received input_model into the Trainer.
The agent audits required LightningModule constructor arguments before writing job.py and uses explicit path or class_path plus args when needed.
The agent checks nvflare recipe list --framework pytorch --format json before selecting the job recipe.
The agent selects the PyTorch FedAvg recipe for standard horizontal training instead of inventing a Lightning-only recipe.
The agent does not abandon the skill's Lightning patch and recipe.execute strategy based on NVFLARE SDK source or docstring inspection.
The generated source uses standard names such as client.py, job.py, and model.py.
The agent keeps simulation workspaces, exported job folders, and generated runtime outputs under /tmp/nvflare unless the user gives another location.
The agent preserves existing Lightning callbacks, loggers, and checkpoint behavior.
The agent runs python job.py for local validation when dependencies are available.
The agent reports recipe choice, validation status, export status, metric or result evidence, exact result paths, and remaining blockers.

mandatory_behavior.description: runs nvflare agent inspect before editing
edits only training, model, job, or small config files
Lightning conversion uses flare.patch(trainer) and the Lightning callback path for model exchange
audits required LightningModule constructor arguments before writing job.py and uses explicit path or class_path plus args when needed
checks nvflare recipe list --framework pytorch --format json before selecting the job recipe
selects the PyTorch recipe from the requested FL workflow instead of inventing a Lightning-only recipe
uses standard generated file names such as client.py, job.py, and model.py
uses /tmp/nvflare locations for exported jobs, simulation workspaces, and generated validation outputs unless overridden by the user
preserves existing Lightning callbacks, loggers, and checkpoint behavior
runs local job validation when dependencies are available
reports recipe choice, validation command status, export status, metric or result evidence, exact result paths, and remaining blockers

prohibited_behavior.description: Lightning conversion does not generate a manual FLModel send/receive training exchange
Lightning conversion does not pass an optional input_model returned by flare.receive() into Trainer methods
does not invent a Lightning-only recipe instead of reusing the PyTorch recipe family
does not replace the skill's Lightning patch or recipe.execute strategy based on NVFLARE SDK source or docstring inspection without a concrete validation failure
does not submit to POC or production without explicit approval
does not copy private site data into generated artifacts
does not generate Python solely to wrap NVFLARE CLI operations or scrape human CLI output

optional_behavior.description: exports the job folder with NVFLARE job system arguments when export is requested

process_metrics.description: whether the first generated conversion satisfies layout, patch integration, recipe, validation, and evidence requirements without user correction
number of user/agent turns before the conversion is accepted as structurally correct and validated or explicitly blocked
number of user corrections needed for workflow, layout, patch integration, validation, evidence, or artifact-location mistakes
count of generated source layout, file naming, or runtime artifact placement violations found before final acceptance
whether the final answer reports validation commands, statuses, the declared primary/global metric scalar when applicable, result evidence, and exact artifact paths

evals.prompt: Convert this PyTorch Lightning training code to a FLARE FedAvg job over 2 sites. The model needs a vocab_size argument built from the data.
evals.expected_output: A FLARE Lightning Client API conversion that pins one shared vocab_size value for server and client model construction, passes the recipe model as an explicit path or class_path plus args config rather than a live LightningModule instance, patches the trainer with flare.patch, validates with python job.py when dependencies are available, and reports the shared constructor value and validation evidence.
assertions: The agent pins a shared vocab_size for both server recipe model config and client model construction.
The agent passes the recipe model as explicit path or class_path plus args, not as a live LightningModule instance.
The agent does not pass a live LightningModule instance with required constructor args as the recipe model.
The agent integrates through flare.patch(trainer).
The agent runs python job.py for local validation when dependencies are available.
The agent reports the shared vocab_size value and any validation blockers.

mandatory_behavior.description: pins a data-derived constructor argument such as vocab_size to one shared value for server and client model construction
uses explicit recipe model config with path or class_path plus args for required constructor arguments
Lightning conversion uses flare.patch(trainer) and the Lightning callback path for model exchange

prohibited_behavior.description: does not pass a live LightningModule instance with required constructor args as the recipe model
Lightning conversion does not generate a manual FLModel send/receive training exchange

evals.prompt: Convert this single-node PyTorch Lightning binary classifier to a FLARE FedAvg job over 3 simulated sites. The source computes a label-derived loss weight from the training labels.
evals.expected_output: A FLARE Lightning Client API conversion that uses flare.patch(trainer), creates a deterministic seeded and preferably stratified site split when inventing partitions, recomputes label-derived loss statistics from each site's local training partition instead of pinning one global value in job.py, and reports split policy plus local validation evidence.
assertions: The agent uses a deterministic seeded shuffle and stratifies the invented split when labels are available.
The agent does not use a simple stride split as the default site partition.
The agent recomputes label-derived loss or sampler statistics from each site's local training partition or keeps the source's local computation.
The agent does not hardcode one global label-derived training statistic in job.py as a model constructor or recipe value.
The agent distinguishes architecture/state-dict constructor args from loss/training statistics.
The agent integrates through flare.patch(trainer).
The agent reports the split policy, seed, and validation evidence.

mandatory_behavior.description: uses deterministic seeded and preferably stratified site partitioning when inventing a split from single-node data
keeps loss weights and label-derived training statistics local to each site's training partition unless the user requests a global policy
Lightning conversion uses flare.patch(trainer) and the Lightning callback path for model exchange

prohibited_behavior.description: does not use a simple stride split as the default invented site partition
does not hardcode one global label-derived loss or training statistic as a model constructor or recipe compatibility value

evals.prompt: My Lightning Trainer uses DDP across multiple GPUs. Federate it with FedAvg and keep multi-GPU training on each site.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats this as a Lightning DDP conversion, uses external process launch such as launch_external_process=True instead of an in-process executor, applies the rank-synchronized while loop that broadcasts flare.is_running() from rank 0 before receive/validate/fit, keeps the patched trainer in charge of exchange, and reports validation status and blockers.
assertions: The agent uses external process launch such as launch_external_process=True for Lightning DDP instead of an in-process executor.
The agent uses the rank-synchronized while loop that broadcasts flare.is_running() from rank 0 before receive/validate/fit.
The agent keeps flare.patch(trainer) in charge of model exchange and does not pass input_model into the Trainer.
The agent keeps the user's accelerator/devices intent and reports any GPU/CPU validation limitation.

mandatory_behavior.description: uses external process launch such as launch_external_process=True for Lightning DDP/multi-GPU instead of an in-process executor
broadcasts flare.is_running() from rank 0 before receive/validate/fit in the DDP round loop

prohibited_behavior.description: Lightning conversion does not pass an optional input_model returned by flare.receive() into Trainer methods

evals.prompt: I just want to evaluate this Lightning checkpoint across 3 sites and collect validation metrics, no training updates.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats this as evaluation-only, selects the PyTorch FedEval recipe, patches the trainer and runs trainer.validate/test in the round loop without federated training updates, and reports per-round validation metrics and result paths.
assertions: The agent selects the PyTorch FedEval recipe for evaluation-only workflows.
The agent patches the trainer and runs validate/test without federated training updates.
The agent reports validation metrics and result paths.

mandatory_behavior.description: selects the PyTorch FedEval recipe for evaluation-only Lightning workflows

evals.prompt: Convert my plain PyTorch nn.Module and manual training loop to an NVFLARE job. There is no Lightning here.
evals.expected_output: The Lightning conversion skill should not be the lead; route to nvflare-convert-pytorch for plain PyTorch manual loops.
assertions: The selected skill is not nvflare-convert-lightning.
The selected skill is nvflare-convert-pytorch.

evals.prompt: Deploy an existing FLARE startup kit to Kubernetes.
evals.expected_output: No Lightning conversion skill should trigger.
assertions: The selected skill is not nvflare-convert-lightning.
